examples: A thimbleful of a neutron star would weigh over 100 million tons.
You don't catch me tasting rum so much, but just a thimbleful for luck, of course, the first chance I have.
Meteor Crater, 40 miles east of Flagstaff, is an out-of-this-world site for those with a thimbleful of imagination.
Italians drink more coffee than almost any country on Earth, which is an impressive feat considering they drink it roughly a thimbleful at a time.
He had tasted it as a prisoner of war in Germany, and the wine, a thimbleful in a mustard jar, was underripe and short on the finish.
